title: zenkerella citrina
intro: Zenkerella citrina, also known as yellow stinkwood, is a tropical plant species native to Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of quassin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Zenkerella citrina include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antioxidant. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
